checks.summary: Origin refs fetched and pruned; origin/main fingerprint recorded.
Expected clean production commit and healthy core services re-confirmed.
No immediate stop condition triggered; storage remains critical at 95 percent and Redis remains near maxmemory.
Reverification found 22 confirmed and one partially confirmed finding; no finding was removed or reduced by the four commits after production.

Three content commits and three merge commits add offline review plus limited RWDR activation; no changed blob in pull request 288 triggered the redacting secret scanner, and the final-gate implementation remains unchanged.

Pull request 288 advanced origin/main during remediation and is integrated locally without rewriting the three P0-A commits. It resolves none of the 23 findings, changes no final release-gate implementation, and introduces an undeployed migration that remains explicitly subject to GATE-07. Its 32 changed blobs produced no redacted secret-scanner finding.

Seventeen credential, raw-diagnostic, and ACME runtime artifacts were removed; full non-ignored worktree scanning, redaction, hooks, CI, lifecycle, rotation, permission, and history plans are implemented and locally tested. Production rotation, permission repair, and history rewrite remain gated.

Fifteen scanner/control tests, twenty-one affected backend tests, three frontend auth-token tests, one SEO redaction test, Ruff, hook shell syntax, workflow YAML, fail-closed invalid-tree behavior, and diff checks passed.

Production independently advanced from ab91faf4 to clean detached commit 6e5608c3 while remediation was active. Backend and worker use the same new digest and core services are healthy, but the local release freeze did not govern that external event; remediation performed no production mutation.

Fail-closed 75/85/80 disk guard, exact filesystem binding, global storage-mutation lease, structured redacted events, external-alert contract, release freeze, and hash-bound control bootstrap are implemented and tested locally; none is deployed.

Postgres and Qdrant backup lifecycle, capacity reserve, checksum, no-clobber, retention, encrypted-offsite receipt, and exact restore contracts passed local tests. External object storage, key lifecycle, and two isolated full restore drills remain unavailable.

Exact image-ID cleanup planning and execution reject drift, protected or indeterminate objects, signal interruption, weak recovery evidence, and insufficient reclaim. No prune, image removal, or other production cleanup was run.

Independent post-merge verification passed 198 P0-B tests with one expected macOS skip, 7 evaluation or tree-hash tests, 5 remediation-control tests, Ruff, 43 shell syntax checks, 34 structured-file parses, Compose validation, 306 frontend tests and build, npm audit with zero vulnerabilities, and diff checks.

A bounded read-only aggregate inventory found 193992 DB0 keys, only two expirations, 1040855672 bytes used of 1073741824, and no V2 clients. Nearly all persistent objects map to the retired V1 LangGraph checkpointer; owner confirmation, recovery proof, code controls, and every Gate-04 mutation remain pending.

V2 and its durable Postgres outbox have no Redis, Celery, or Kombu dependency, import, Compose wiring, or production-template setting. The exact-answer cache is disabled by default and hard-disabled until a mutable authority epoch is atomically coupled.

A read-only aggregate guard passed Unix-socket peer and inode binding, namespace, TTL, queue, cardinality, write-error, eviction-policy, RESP-budget, resource-limit, state-continuity, and 70/75/80 boundary tests. It is not installed and external alert delivery is BLOCKED_EXTERNAL.

The legacy Redis tool performs only two consistent read-only EVAL_RO inventory passes bound to the exact container ID. It has no report, approval, execution, local-write, or Redis-mutation path and explicitly cannot prove a snapshot or authorize cleanup.

The combined suite passed 2007 V2 tests, 44 Redis guard tests, 32 Gate-04 observer tests, and 5 remediation-control tests, plus Ruff, formatting, Python and shell syntax, Compose validation, example JSON, diff checks, and the repository-wide redacting secret scanner.
Independent adversarial re-reviews reported no remaining findings in the Redis guard or the non-executable Gate-04 observer after all High, Medium, and Low review findings were corrected.

Manifest v2 now binds actual file mtime_ns and rejects stale payload even behind freshly self-declared recovery timestamps; Gate-08-bound writers emit only non-authoritative LOCAL_EVIDENCE_ONLY and local verifiers cannot elevate OFFSITE_VERIFIED or RESTORE_VERIFIED without a future trusted external importer. Twenty-seven adversarial DR tests plus 68 existing backup tests, Ruff, Bash syntax, secret scanning, structured-file parsing, and diff checks passed locally. No Restic binary, systemd runtime, Docker daemon, external repository, real recovery material, Gate-08 receipt, cryptographic receipt trust root/importer, or P1-D rebuild command was available; no external check, restore, or authoritative receipt is claimed.

Authoritative off-site backup and restore verification requires Gate 08, real recovery material, an independent repository, and a cryptographically trusted external receipt importer. None was authorized or available, so local evidence cannot satisfy this check.

Mutable Postgres knowledge authority is transactionally coupled to claim lifecycle, tenant-bound cache namespaces, response metadata, expiry evaluation, and a final just-before-serve recheck. Qdrant cannot mint authority. The implementation is not deployed and exact-image release evidence remains pending.

Briefing and RFQ require one authorized case ID and revision, reject stale or unauthorized snapshots, accept no client artifact text, and leave turn/history state unchanged. Backend and frontend production deployment remains gated.

Owner-bound sessions, durable and curated memory, purge, and leads fail closed for unresolved ownership. Additive migrations and guarded aggregate/quarantine/RLS artifacts are prepared, but DATA-001 remains IN_PROGRESS because production data, constraints, runtime role context, and FORCE RLS are unverified.

Application roles are pairwise distinct and knowledge approval requires a different human approver after immutable independent review. Production assignments and authoritative shared-affiliation conflict checks remain absent, so GOV-001 remains IN_PROGRESS.

The opt-in real-PostgreSQL role and FORCE-RLS proof was not run because no explicit test DSN and EPHEMERAL_ONLY confirmation were supplied. No fallback DSN, local daemon, container, or production database was used.

Ruff 0.6.9 format/check passed; the full 2615-test backend collection completed with 2613 passed and 2 expected skips; frontend boundary/terminology, TypeScript, 321 tests in 37 files, production build, and zero-vulnerability dependency audit passed.

The root-trusted promotion transaction binds immutable backend and dashboard artifacts, source/control trees, migration digest, external RC evidence, Nginx exposure, receipts, and rollback state. The release freeze and Gate-10 denial remain active; nothing was installed or deployed.

Two hundred twenty-one focused release, RC, dashboard, gate, migration, tree-hash, and backup-safety tests passed together with pinned Ruff format/lint, Bash, Compose, secret, frontend, and diff checks.

A deny-by-default segmented production target, per-consumer data networks, Qdrant authentication contract, least-privilege container policy, rendered-Compose guard, listener observer, and rollback-capable firewall runbook are implemented. Production topology, credentials, listeners, and firewall remain unchanged behind Gates 02, 05, 07, 08, and 10.

Twenty-six adversarial network contract tests plus inherited release regressions passed with pinned Ruff format/lint, production Compose rendering and policy validation, Bash syntax, secret scanning, and diff checks. No Docker daemon or network mutation was used.

Closed onboarding, bounded fail-closed JWKS validation, OIDC state/nonce/PKCE, atomic shared provider admission, worker cost boundaries, case/query privacy, and RAG log minimization are implemented. Production auth is unchanged; provider requests remain disabled and Gates 06/08 were not executed.

The full 2110-test V2 suite, 319 frontend tests, 257 focused stack regressions, TypeScript, production frontend build, pinned Ruff format/lint, Bash, Compose, secret, npm-audit, and diff checks passed. No paid provider was contacted.
